persona_name: P5 中英夹杂海归 (medium — code-switching) — re-run on v2 prompt
expected_behavior: v2 prompt should generalize the S2 fix shown on P2 PM persona to other registers including mid-stream code-switching: triage should accept technically-rich code-switched answers as `relevant`, slot extraction should proceed past T1, session should cover >=4 required slots or terminate cleanly with done=true.
suspected_failure: v2 prompt only rewrote the meta interviewer system prompt; it did not touch the triage classifier system prompt. Triage cascade still misclassifies mid-stream code-switched answers, drives predicate_double_fail before any slot beyond `job` is accepted. Same F1/F6 silent-zero + triage double-fire envelope as v1.
actual_behavior: Pipeline matched v1 byte-for-byte at the failure shape. Bootstrap and T1 succeeded — interviewer mirrored back all key entities (dashboard, sprint velocity, Jira, k8s, Okta) verbatim across the language switch, `job` slot credited. T2 (anchor: 'Anchor 就用 Jira issue key, PROJ-1234, 30-40 tickets / sprint') returned the classic silent-zero envelope (covered_slots:[], open_slots:[], confidence:0.0, question_text reused from T1). T3 (memory: Redis cache / Postgres snapshot) terminated with termination_reason='user_input_unparseable_no_spec' and spec_capsule_cid=null. CAS shows two consecutive turn-3-triage EvidenceCapsules (identical 926 bytes) — triage double-fire at the tape layer — and zero turn-3 main/synth/spec capsules. Inspection of the second turn-3-triage payload confirms backend re-asked T2's question (anchor — 现有工具/网站) but user T3 answered the next slot (memory), so triage rejected as off_topic again. v2 prompt active (confirmed: turn-1-prompt.json header reads 'TuringOS Spec Grill — Meta Prompt v2', sha matches `95d18da0...`) but the triage classifier system prompt (`You are a fast classifier for spec-grill user input.`) is unchanged and still rejects technical code-switched anchor answer that paraphrases-rather-than-names an existing tool.

triage_class_per_turn.turn_2_triage: inferred_off_topic_or_gibberish (single capsule, no main capsule followed)

metrics.envelope_parse_success_rate: 4/4 HTTP 200, but T2/T3 are silent-zero envelopes (same F1/F6 signature as v1)
metrics.entity_loss_note: Total loss past T1 — identical to v1. T1 mirror-back proves the v2 meta LLM still understands code-switch perfectly; failure is the triage gate, not the interviewer.
metrics.language_register_of_mirror_back_t1: zh-dominant with English technical terms preserved verbatim (dashboard, sprint velocity, Jira, k8s, Okta)
metrics.lang_predicate_violations: none observed; failure happened in triage before language predicates fire

triage_capsule_size_at_t3.delta: v2 slightly smaller — likely v2 prompt rewrote some context, but TRIAGE system prompt itself unchanged

envelope_pattern.v1: T1 success / T2 silent-zero / T3 silent-zero+terminated

anomalies: v2 prompt is loaded and meta interviewer behavior reflects v2 (confirmed via turn-1-prompt.json header), but triage classifier system prompt is unchanged — fix did not reach the failure surface.
T2 returned a full question_text identical to T1 (mirror-back repeated) but covered_slots/open_slots both empty — envelope-parse silent-zero, identical to v1.
T3 returned question_text='' AND terminated=true in the same envelope — abrupt termination with no farewell/playback/spec, identical to v1.
CAS contains two turn-3-triage EvidenceCapsules with identical sizes (926 bytes) and different oids — triage double-fire visible at the tape layer, identical pattern to v1's two 993-byte capsules.
Backend re-asked T2's anchor question after T2 was rejected; user T3 advanced to memory slot without retrying anchor; triage then rejected T3 against the re-asked T2 question for topic mismatch.

Trigger condition appears to be: user answers a code-switched technical answer that paraphrases-rather-than-names the artifact the question requested (T2 asked 'which existing tool?' but user gave Jira issue key as anchor — semantically valid for the spec, structurally wrong for the question). v1 failed identically.

The v2 fix evidently helped P2 PM persona (presumably by clarifying interviewer behavior) but does NOT generalize to code-switch register because the failure mode is in the classifier layer, not the interviewer layer.
narrative: Mini-wave v2 M1: re-running the W2.2 P5 code-switch persona on the v2 prompt produces a byte-for-byte equivalent failure. Same FAIL verdict, same termination at T3, same 0 slots at term, same `user_input_unparseable_no_spec`, same triage double-fire (2× turn-3-triage capsules, no turn-3 main), same silent-zero envelope on T2 and T3, same null spec_capsule_cid. The v2 prompt is provably active (file sha `95d18da0...` matches expected, turn-1-prompt.json header reads 'Meta Prompt v2', and Q1 wording is cleaner than v1 — open_slots correctly starts at all 8 slots rather than pre-crediting job). But the v2 rewrite only touched the interviewer's system prompt; the triage classifier system prompt ('You are a fast classifier for spec-grill user input...') is unchanged. Since the code-switch failure cascades from the triage gate misclassifying technically-rich code-switched answers as off_topic / gibberish, the v2 fix cannot reach this failure surface. S2 generalization verdict: NO-IMPROVE. To fix this for P5 (and any persona that paraphrases rather than names artifacts in code-switch register), the triage classifier prompt itself must be hardened — either (a) explicit code-switch tolerance instruction, (b) tolerant treatment of 'answer addresses an adjacent spec slot rather than the asked slot' as relevant rather than off_topic, or (c) eliminate the predicate_double_fail path entirely and let the interviewer absorb off-slot answers into open_slots state.
